Rimal Utilities Co. (Listed on Najd Securities Exchange “NSE‑KSA”; Issuer ID: RUCL‑202) — Operational/Cyber Incident Report, 2026‑05‑30 18:40 KSA
Summary: A routine access review revealed a short‑lived exposure of a non‑production document folder hosted by Barq Cloud Services WLL; logs indicate a single automated scan and no confirmed download beyond internal IPs.
Data scope: primarily vendor invoices and maintenance SOPs; two image files were identified as scans of vendor drivers’ Iqama cards associated with delivery gate passes.
PDPL notice: Although the images were in a test folder, they constitute personal data under the Kingdom PDPL; the files were quarantined and erased, and we initiated a DPIA addendum with the Supreme Data & AI Authority (SDAA) transfer desk noting no cross‑border transfer is planned.
Breach containment: Access keys were rotated, external indexing blocked, and Barq Cloud validated bucket policies; Amthal Cyber Response JV confirmed no lateral movement into production.
Public information: Service bulletins on 1445‑02‑11H and 2023‑05‑03G about scheduled desalination maintenance remain accurate and were previously posted to the NSE‑KSA Market Announcements Archive.
One non‑public item was present: a draft issuer notice proposing to postpone the Q2 results announcement by up to one week; this draft has not been submitted to the NSE‑KSA portal or the Kingdom Markets Authority (KMA).
Materiality/market conduct: The draft does not alter guidance and is pending Board review; disclosure will follow KMA Listing Rules if a postponement is confirmed, and no selective briefings have occurred.
Internal controls: References in system logs to “insider list” and “blackout window” relate to archived training content and not to any live trading restriction at this time.
Identifiers and forms (placeholders only): CR No. 101‑XX‑0000; National ID field example 0000‑0000‑0000 (invalid); Form: Incident Intake v2.3 — these labels are generic and not linked to real records.
Customer impact: No customer accounts, billing files, or live operational telemetry were in the exposed folder; desalination and power distribution operations remain nominal.
Third‑party coordination: Vendor Barq Cloud Services WLL provided an attestation of log retention; no SDAA permit is required given no international transfer or processor change.
Issuer workflow: If the postponement proceeds, the Company will pre‑notify the NSE‑KSA Market Announcements team and file within the prescribed disclosure window on the next trading day.
Contact points: compliance@rimal‑utilities.example (role mailbox); PD inquiries via the public helpline listed on our website under Privacy Requests.
Next steps: Finalize DPIA addendum, reconfirm data mapping for test environments, and present the draft notice to the Disclosure Committee for a go/no‑go decision within five business days.
